scene_text: Number 9, Neutrino, predicted 1930, found 1956.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi digital illustration. Deep black space with a cold blue-purple gradient and precise distant star field. Center: a holographic particle classification display — a glowing teal data card floating in zero gravity, showing a faint translucent sphere at its center, barely visible, labeled by abstract circular emblems (no text). Thin technical grid lines frame the display. The particle at center is a near-invisible pale blue orb — a ghost signal on a detection screen, just barely registering above background noise. Cold electric blue and teal color palette. Sharp, precise linework, bold black outlines on the holographic frame. Hard light sources, no warm magical glow. Technical, clinical, scientific. No text anywhere.

scene_text: In 1930, the fundamental laws of physics were on the verge of total collapse.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Deep black background with cold blue-purple tones and a precise technical star field. Center: a massive holographic physics framework — an enormous interconnected web of equations represented as glowing geometric nodes and connecting lines, like a circuit schematic of the universe's laws. The entire structure is glitching violently — nodes flickering offline, connection lines sparking and breaking, error-state red pulses rippling through the network. Sections of the grid fragment and fall into static. The breakdown is systematic and catastrophic. Electric sparks and data corruption patterns scatter from every failing node. Cold precise colors: electric blue, warning red, static white. Bold outlines on structural elements. No fantasy elements — this is a system failure in a scientific framework. No text anywhere.

scene_text: Scientists studying beta decay, a process where a radioactive atom emits an electron, noticed something completely impossible.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Dark navy-black background, precise cold star field. Center: a technical diagram of beta decay — a large cartoon nucleus (cluster of red protons and grey neutrons, bold black outlines) firing a glowing blue electron from its core at high velocity, leaving a sharp electric trail. Energy waves ripple outward in precise concentric technical arcs. Left side: a simple stick figure scientist — basic stick body, round head, a small pair of dot eyes showing intense concentration — standing at a control panel covered in oscilloscope readouts and Geiger counter dials showing unexpected readings, expression shifting to confused alarm. The instrumentation reacts to the impossible result. Cold teal-blue palette, hard technical lighting, precise linework. No text anywhere.

scene_text: When they measured the energy before and after the decay, the numbers didn't add up.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Dark lab-in-space environment, cold blue-black gradient. Center: a simple stick figure scientist stands between two large scientific measurement instruments — one on each side. The LEFT instrument is a glowing energy meter with its readout needle pointing high (full energy, bright blue). The RIGHT instrument is the same meter after the reaction — its needle slumped to the left, reading significantly lower, the readout glowing dim and warning-orange. A connecting diagram between the two meters shows an arrow indicating the energy gap — a missing chunk. The stick figure stares at both readouts simultaneously, its simple dot-eyes wide, arms spread in disbelief. Technical precision instruments: dials, oscilloscope waves, numerical readout panels (abstract symbols only, no text). Cold clinical colors with warning orange for the deficit. No text anywhere.

scene_text: Energy was simply vanishing into the void. For physicists, the conservation of energy is sacred.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Deep black void of space, minimal cold star field. Center: a particle detector track diagram — a scientific bubble chamber or cloud chamber visualization showing incoming particle tracks. The tracks enter from the left as bright glowing blue lines, reach a reaction point, and the outgoing tracks on the right side account for less energy — one track simply stops mid-path and vanishes into empty space, its line fading from bright blue to nothing. A large energy conservation equation symbol (abstract — no text, shown as a circular balance icon) hovers to the upper right, crossed by a warning red line indicating violation. The void on the right is absolute black. Cold clinical color palette. Bold precise outlines on the tracks. Technically illustrates missing energy as a disappearing particle track. No text anywhere.

scene_text: It is the bedrock of our understanding of reality. To suggest it was wrong was astronomical heresy.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Deep black space, cold distant stars. Center: a colossal titanium-and-steel sci-fi monolith — a massive rectangular structure made of interconnected metal framework, clean geometric precision, glowing blue energy conduits running through its structure like veins. It represents the foundation of physics as an engineered construct. This structure is beginning to fail: stress fractures appear as sharp white cracks along structural joints, blue energy bleeds from the breaks, and metal fragments begin to drift free in zero gravity. The scale is immense and imposing — it feels like watching a space station's core framework buckle. Cold steel-blue and white color palette. Hard industrial lighting. Bold black outlines on all structural elements. Pure sci-fi architecture, no fantasy. No text anywhere.

scene_text: Desperate to save the laws of physics, Austrian physicist Wolfgang Pauli proposed what he called a desperate remedy.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Dark lab environment in space, cold blue-black tones. Center: a simple stick figure — basic round head, stick body, wearing a tiny suggested lab coat (a simple white rectangle shape, no detail) — hunched urgently over a floating holographic drafting table. The table displays a glowing teal technical schematic: abstract particle interaction diagrams, force vectors, and reaction pathway lines. The stick figure's posture is tense and frantic, sweat lines radiating from its head, expression showing desperate determination — wide dot-eyes, stressed mouth. Surrounding it, holographic readouts show the failing energy balance problem in abstract diagram form (warning-orange indicators). The stick figure is actively sketching a new theoretical particle into the schematic with a glowing stylus. Hard technical lighting, bold outlines. No text anywhere.

scene_text: He wrote a letter to a group of scientists, famously addressing them as dear radioactive ladies and gentlemen.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Deep space, cold blue-purple star field. Center: a holographic data transmission beam shoots through space — a blue tractor-beam-style corridor of light carrying a glowing message capsule (smooth sci-fi design, sealed, with an abstract radioactive trefoil emblem on its face — not text, just the iconic symbol). The beam connects to a cluster of floating science stations in the distance — each station has a small stick figure at its window or terminal, watching the incoming transmission with curious and surprised expressions (dot eyes wide, simple open mouths). The radioactive trefoil symbol glows prominently on the message capsule. The scene feels like a scientific broadcast across a research network. Cold teal-blue palette, clean technical lines, bold outlines. No text anywhere.

scene_text: and hypothesized the existence of a ghost particle.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Dark space laboratory environment, cold blue-black gradient. Center: a large particle detector screen — a circular scope display showing the paths of all known particles as bright clean lines. But threading through the center: one almost-invisible trajectory — a faint dotted line passing through everything and continuing out the other side, leaving no reaction, no secondary particles, no energy deposit. The dotted line is barely perceptible against the background. It is a theoretical annotation — a prediction — drawn in dashed pale blue. Above it, a small holographic question mark icon (as an abstract shape, not text) pulses with uncertainty. The detector hardware is technical and angular: boxy, with glowing indicator lights and precise measurement grids. Cold clinical colors. Bold outlines on all hardware. No text anywhere.

scene_text: This theoretical particle had to have no electrical charge, practically zero mass, and almost zero interaction with normal matter.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Dark sci-fi space background, cold precise star field. Center: a technical particle property schematic — three circular instrument readout panels arranged in a row. First panel: CHARGE readout — the needle sits perfectly at zero, an empty circle diagram showing no charge. Second panel: MASS readout — the needle barely lifts from zero, a featherweight scale showing near-nothing. Third panel: INTERACTION readout — a particle collision diagram where all interaction probability bars are flat at zero. All three panels show extreme readings at the null end of the scale. Below the panels: a faint dotted-line particle trace passes through a dense field of other particles (solid colored spheres with bold outlines) without a single interaction. The panels are angular, industrial sci-fi instrument design. Cold technical colors: teal, blue-white, zero-reading grey. No text anywhere.

scene_text: It would simply carry the missing energy away, completely undetected.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Dark space, cold star field. Center: a particle physics event diagram — a collision vertex at the center where particles collide and products scatter outward as glowing blue tracks. All the tracks are accounted for except one: a gap in the energy balance. A single dotted-line trajectory exits the collision vertex and travels off-screen — invisible, untracked, carrying energy with it. A sci-fi energy ledger display to the side shows two columns: particles IN and particles OUT — the OUT column is short by one entry, a missing line in the tally shown as an empty gap. The collision hardware around the vertex is a cylindrical particle detector chamber, technical and precise. Cold clinical blue palette. Bold precise outlines on all physical tracks. The missing track is deliberately invisible. No text anywhere.

scene_text: Pauli himself was horrified by his own creation, stating, I have done a terrible thing. I have postulated a particle that cannot be detected.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Dark cold space lab, deep blue-black, minimal stars. Center: a simple stick figure — round head, stick body, suggested lab coat — sits slumped on a lab stool, face buried in hands. Radiating from its head: sharp horror lines and sweat drops, expression of absolute dread. In front of it, a floating holographic display shows the particle it created — a pale faint dotted circle, nearly invisible on the screen, the undetectable. The display has a large warning-red border around it indicating an unsolvable problem. The contrast is stark: a solid visible stick figure creator looking at its intangible, undetectable creation. The creation hovers on the screen, mocking in its invisibility. Cold blue-black color palette, hard light from the holographic screen illuminating the stick figure from the front. Bold outlines on all physical elements, almost no outline on the theoretical particle. No text anywhere.

scene_text: He even bet a case of champagne that humanity would never prove its existence. He lost that bet.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Cold space environment. Left side: a simple stick figure — lab coat, confident posture, a smug expression (closed smile, half-lidded dot eyes) — points definitively at a floating bet contract hologram showing an abstract symbol for champagne and an X symbol (wager). Right side, decades later, the same stick figure — now hunched, head bowed, clear defeated expression (downturned mouth, drooping eyes) — next to a floating holographic confirmation screen with a clear CHECK MARK icon (not text) showing the particle WAS detected. Between them a stylized timeline arrow spans the scene. At the bottom: a cartoon champagne bottle floating in zero-g, its cork having already blown, bubbles drifting outward in space. Clean sci-fi environment: dark space, holographic blue teal screens, bold precise outlines. No text anywhere.

scene_text: it took 26 years. In 1956, during Project Poltergeist.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Dark cold space, precise technical star field. Center: a sleek sci-fi mission badge/emblem floats in space — a hexagonal or circular emblem with a ghost-signal waveform icon at its center (a faint dotted sine wave, representing the elusive neutrino signal) and a radioactive trefoil in the corner. The emblem radiates a cold authoritative glow. Behind it: a timeline display showing 26 ticks of progress — represented as 26 illuminated nodes on a horizontal progress bar, each one lighting up in sequence, the final node blazing bright indicating success. The overall aesthetic is a government science mission — cold, official, precise. Teal and cold blue color palette. Bold clean outlines, technical precision. No fantasy, no warmth — this is classified science. No text anywhere.

scene_text: physicists Clyde Cowan and Frederick Rines set up a massive detector next to the Savannah River nuclear reactor.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Industrial sci-fi environment — cold blue-grey color palette. Two simple stick figures — both with dot eyes showing focused determination, both wearing suggested lab coats — work together to connect cables into a massive boxy detector machine. The machine towers over them: a hulking technical structure with rows of cylindrical sensor modules, bundled cable conduits, and glowing blue detection screens showing abstract waveforms. To its right, a nuclear reactor tower looms: a cylindrical structure with steam vents and a yellow-green radiation glow emanating from its core. Scale is emphasized — the stick figures are tiny against the machines. The setting is underground and industrial: concrete walls hinted with grid-pattern texture. Bold outlines, hard directional lighting from the reactor and detector screens. Cold grey-blue-teal palette. No text anywhere.

scene_text: By looking for the infinitesimally rare moment a neutrino actually collided with a proton, they
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Dark particle physics environment, cold black with teal-blue accents. Center: a close-up of a cylindrical particle detector interior — a cross-section view showing the detector's inner chamber. Thousands of neutrino trajectory lines (extremely faint dotted paths) pass straight through the detector volume without any reaction, filling the chamber. But one — just one — shows a collision vertex: two secondary tracks branching outward at angles from a single point, the signature of a genuine interaction. That one interaction point is highlighted with a crisp bright glow, a hard circle indicator marking it. The geometry is precise: before-and-after track angles shown with technical accuracy. A probability fraction display on the outer detector frame shows a tiny fraction (abstract ratio icon — no numbers). Bold outlines on the detector hardware, faint lines on the neutrino paths, bright on the collision vertex. No text anywhere.

scene_text: finally proved the ghost was real.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Dark space lab, suddenly lit by a triumphant display. Center: a large mission control monitor screen showing a confirmed detection readout — the detector scope screen now has one clear, undeniable bright blip on it, glowing electric white-blue, perfectly centered. Above it, a holographic CONFIRMED status icon (a bold checkmark symbol, not text) blazes green. Two simple stick figures stand to either side of the screen — both have wide dot-eyes and raised arms, expressions of pure elated shock. One has fists raised in triumph. The screen illuminates them with a blue-white glow from the front. Behind them, banks of other screens and instruments glow in confirmation. After 26 years, the signal is real. Cold colors now lit with bright triumphant highlights. Bold clean outlines. No text anywhere.

scene_text: But the discovery of the neutrino unveiled a deeply unsettling reality about our place in the cosmos. Because they barely interact with matter, neutrinos
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Vast cold space — deep navy-to-black gradient, very sparse distant stars, atmosphere of immense emptiness. Center: a cosmic scale visualization — an entire solar system shown from a distance, planets orbiting. Through the entire solar system, in every direction, countless neutrino flux lines flow as faint blue-white dashed trajectories, unobstructed by any planet or star, passing through everything as if it doesn't exist. The scale is humbling — the solar system looks small and permeable. The neutrino flux fills all of space equally everywhere. A cold scientific beauty to the scene. No warmth — this is not wonder, it is the unsettling recognition of complete cosmic transparency. Deep blue-black palette, precise cold lines. Bold outlines on the planetary bodies. No text anywhere.

scene_text: treat solid objects as if they don't exist. Right now, approximately 100 trillion neutrinos are passing through your body every single second.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Dark space with cold technical tones. Center: a full-body scientific scan diagram — a simple stick figure shown in a medical-scan cross-section style (like a particle physics detector cross-section). Through the stick figure's entire body, a dense torrent of blue-white particle flux lines penetrates — hundreds of tight parallel dashed lines entering from above and exiting below, completely unimpeded. The flux density is overwhelming — the figure is saturated with invisible particle traffic. On either side, flux density counters (abstract dial readouts) register enormous numbers in abstract scale indicators. The stick figure itself stands still, arms at sides, expression showing unsettled existential realization — eyes wide open, slightly downturned mouth. Cold blue-white particle lines against dark background. Bold outlines on the figure. Technical, clinical, precise. No text anywhere.

scene_text: originating from the thermonuclear furnace of the sun, are passing through your body every single second.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Deep black space. Left: a technical cross-section of the Sun — a circular cutaway diagram showing its internal structure: a bright white plasma core labeled with abstract fusion reaction icons (circle bursting into smaller circles), surrounded by layers in radiative and convective zones shown with distinct color bands (white core, yellow radiative zone, orange convective zone). From the core, neutrino flux lines (dashed blue-white lines) propagate outward through all solar layers and continue straight outward into space — passing through the Sun's own body without interaction. Right side: the flux lines continue across space toward a distant small Earth. This is a scientific diagram of solar neutrino production, not a fantasy sun. Cold technical illustration style, precise color coding for each solar layer. Bold clean outlines. No text anywhere.

scene_text: They are passing through your eyes, your heart, and your brain.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Cold clinical environment — dark blue-black, medical scan aesthetic. Center: a simple stick figure shown in a transparent body scan view — a clean outlined figure with three highlighted internal regions: eyes (two glowing dot circles in the head), heart (a simplified anatomical heart shape in the chest cavity), and brain (a rounded brain outline in the skull). Through each of these organs — and through the entire body — a continuous horizontal stream of parallel blue-white neutrino flux lines passes unobstructed, left to right, touching nothing. Where the flux lines intersect each highlighted organ, they do not slow, deflect, or interact. The stick figure's expression: quiet, slightly disturbed, staring forward. The body scan frame around it is clinical and technical — scanner rings, measurement indicators. Bold outlines on the figure. Cold blue-white palette. No text anywhere.

scene_text: They pass through the entire earth without hitting a single atom. You are completely transparent to the universe.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Deep black space. Center: a scientific cross-section diagram of Earth — a perfect half-circle cutaway showing precisely labeled layers in distinct colors: thin blue-grey crust, warm brown mantle, dark red outer core, bright white inner core. Each layer is rendered as a clean technical diagram band with sharp boundaries. From far left of the frame to far right, a dense field of parallel blue-white neutrino flux lines cuts through the planet in perfectly straight horizontal lines, entering through the crust and exiting the opposite crust with absolutely zero deflection. Every layer is equally penetrated. The flux lines do not care about iron, rock, or plasma — they continue undeviated. The cross-section has a precise technical schematic aesthetic: clean line weights, distinct layer colors. Bold black outlines on all layer boundaries. Cold scientific palette. No text anywhere.

scene_text: When a massive star goes supernova across the galaxy, it releases
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Deep black space filled with a dense star field for galactic scale. Center: a massive stellar explosion in progress — a supernova rendered as a hard-science astrophysical event. The dying star's core is a brilliant white-blue point of collapse — a forming neutron star. Around it, perfect concentric shockwave rings expand outward: innermost ring white-hot plasma, second ring ionized blue-white gas, outer rings deep violet fading to dark. The ejected stellar material forms precise jet streams and expanding shell arcs in whites and blues — not fantasy debris but scientifically accurate plasma shell geometry. The scale is enormous against the galactic star field. Cold high-energy colors: white, electric blue, deep violet, hard shadows. Precise clean linework, bold outlines on shockwave rings. Astrophysical, not magical. No text anywhere.

scene_text: a shockwave of neutrinos that washes over our planet hours before the light even reaches us.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Deep black space, minimal star field. A side-view scientific diagram showing two wavefronts racing across space toward a small cartoon Earth on the right. FRONT wave: a nearly invisible translucent dashed-line wavefront — the neutrino shockwave — rendered as a faint blue-white boundary that barely registers, sweeping through space ahead of everything. BEHIND it, a visible gap of empty space, then the SECOND wave: the electromagnetic light wavefront — a solid glowing white-orange line, bright and clearly visible, moving slower. The gap between the two wavefronts is deliberately wide and labeled with abstract duration symbols (clock icon, not text). The Earth on the right side is unaware — small, calm. The neutrino wave is about to reach it; the light wave is far behind. The diagram is scientific and precise — like a physics textbook illustration in sci-fi style. Soft edges on neutrino front, bold outlines on light wave and Earth. No text anywhere.

scene_text: We live in a silent, invisible storm of phantom particles, a constant reminder
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Vast cold space — deep navy-black gradient, very sparse stars, atmosphere of immense cosmic scale. Center: a simple stick figure floats alone in open space — no spacesuit indicated, just the figure itself, arms slightly out at sides. Its expression: contemplative, slightly unsettled, eyes looking slightly downward and forward. Through and around the stick figure — in every direction, from every angle — near-invisible flux trajectories flow. They are rendered at maximum subtlety: the faintest possible pale blue-white dashed lines, barely distinguishable from the dark background. They come from above, below, left, right, diagonally — a constant omnidirectional storm that the figure cannot see, feel, or stop. The figure is tiny against the vast universe. The scene is simultaneously beautiful and deeply lonely. Bold outlines only on the stick figure. Minimal rendering on the flux lines. Cold vast color palette. No text anywhere.

scene_text: that the solid world we perceive is little more than an illusion.
prompt: Clean 2D sci-fi illustration. Deep black space. Split-frame composition with a sharp vertical boundary at center-frame. LEFT HALF: a solid-looking titanium block — rendered as opaque, substantial, with a precise clean surface finish and bold black outlines — it looks completely real and impenetrable. A scale indicator shows it as dense matter. RIGHT HALF: the same block revealed at atomic scale — a scientific zoom diagram. The interior is almost entirely empty: tiny nucleus dots (small red and blue spheres, bold outlines) separated by enormous voids. Electron orbital paths trace thin rings around each nucleus. Between every atom: vast black emptiness. Blue-white neutrino flux lines flow effortlessly and invisibly through all the empty space between atoms, unimpeded. A sharp diagonal dividing line between the two views creates the reveal. The left side is bold and opaque; the right side is mostly black void with tiny glowing nodes. Cold technical color palette. Scientific precision. No text anywhere.
